quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 11 Quiz 12: Trigonometric Functions — Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 11: Trigonometric Functions — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: A sector has radius $10$ cm and central angle $108^{\circ}$. Find the sector area.
An arc of length $14\pi$ cm subtends a central angle of $\frac{7\pi}{6}$ radians. Find the radius.
Find the coordinates of the point on the unit circle corresponding to $\frac{5\pi}{3}$.
questions.explanation: QIV: cosine positive, sine negative. Reference $\pi/3$: $\cos=1/2$, $\sin=\sqrt{3}/2$. Coordinates: $(1/2, -\sqrt{3}/2)$.

questions.question: For $y = 4\sin(3x) - 1$, find the amplitude, period, range, and $y$-intercept.
A sine function has amplitude $6$, period $\pi$, phase shift $\frac{\pi}{4}$ right, and midline $y = -2$. Write the equation.
Find the vertical asymptotes of $y = \cot(x - \frac{\pi}{3})$ in $[0, 2\pi]$.
A spring oscillates with frequency $2.5$ Hz and amplitude $3$ cm. Write the equation using cosine (starting at max) and find the period.
questions.explanation: $b = 2\pi f = 5\pi$. $T = 1/f = 0.4$ sec. Starts at max → cosine: $y = 3\cos(5\pi t)$.

questions.question: A Ferris wheel has radius $25$ ft, center $30$ ft above ground, and completes one revolution in $40$ seconds. At $t=0$, a rider is at the bottom. Write the height function $h(t)$ using cosine.
questions.explanation: Midline $= 30$, Amp $= 25$, Period $40 \to b = \pi/20$. Bottom at $t=0 \to$ negative cosine.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 11: sector area from degrees, finding radius from arc length, unit circle coordinates, evaluating tangent, key sinusoidal features, writing equations, asymptotes of reciprocal functions, inverse trig compositions, SHM, and Ferris wheel modeling.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) Convert degrees to radians before using sector/arc formulas, (2) $r = s/\theta$ — solve for radius, (3) For inverse trig compositions, draw a right triangle, (4) Ferris wheel: midline = center height, amplitude = radius, bottom start = negative cosine.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) $A = \frac{1}{2}r^2\theta$ requires radians, (2) Writing equations: $b = 2\pi/T$, phase shift direction, (3) $\cot$ asymptotes where $\sin = 0$, $\tan$ asymptotes where $\cos = 0$, (4) $\tan(\arcsin(x))$ — use Pythagorean identity to find the missing side.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 11 fundamentals: (1) $s = r\theta$ and $A = \frac{1}{2}r^2\theta$ require radians — convert degrees first, (2) Unit circle: $(\cos\theta, \sin\theta)$, use reference angles and quadrant signs, (3) $y = a\sin(b(x-h)) + k$ — amplitude $|a|$, period $2\pi/|b|$, phase shift $h$, midline $k$, (4) Inverse trig returns principal values in restricted ranges.
